H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong. Distribution shows rightward skew without bimodality; qualitative data (n=29) confirms overstimulation relief with settle time around 10 minutes — 66% reported positive change. YouTube views=30 with NULL retention indicates listener acquisition through non-platform channels. AutoGluon predicted=6.86, slightly below observed mean, suggesting consistent efficacy signal. No cross-volume divergence as only VOL001 has data.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble works reliably for over-stimulation — most listeners settle within about 10 minutes and feel noticeable improvement. YouTube shows low discovery but strong word-of-mouth efficacy.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong. Slight decay from L1 (mean −0.23) with 65% positive rate. Qualitative reports (n=21) show balanced rhythm opinions with settle times of 10 minutes. YouTube views=5 — minimal discovery. AutoGluon predicted score shows no cross-bucket routing. Single-volume data (VOL001) — no divergence to assess.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall performs slightly below Pebble but still helps most listeners with overstimulation. The rhythm feels calming to those who respond.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Maintains efficacy slightly above series mean with 64% positive. Qualitative data (n=23) indicates rhythm perceived as balanced/comfortable. YouTube views=8 — low but highest among OS variants. AutoGluon flags minor cross-bucket to motivation zone. Single-volume (VOL001) pattern consistent.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier maintains strong efficacy for over-stimulation relief with a balanced, settling rhythm — good consistency.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong. Bimodal distribution with peaks at 0–3 and 8–10 suggests population mismatch — some listeners report no effect while others experience significant improvement. Qualitative (n=23): 10/23 report moderate improvement, 9/23 settle within 10 minutes. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.84, aligning closely with observed. Drift=0.665 indicates evolving analytical framing — not protocol failure.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow helps many with anxiety but some listeners don't respond at all — suggests a population mismatch worth investigating.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. Tighter distribution than L1 (std=1.62 vs 1.93) with 67% positive. Qualitative (n=20): faster settling (~5 min) but lower qualitative positivity — 9 liked music, 5 neutral. YouTube views=6. AutoGluon flags cross-bucket routing to motivation/mood. Drift=0.652 — notable analytical shift.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage works faster than Meadow for anxiety but with slightly lower overall effect. Some listeners stay neutral on the music.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful. Distribution shows spread across mid-to-high range. Qualitative (n=20): 10/20 settled at ~10 minutes, 8 reported noticeable improvement. Music opinion split: 8 liked, 5 neutral. YouTube views=4. Drift=0.695 — analytical framing has shifted notably.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley takes longer to work (around 10 minutes) but still helps most anxiety sufferers. Music opinions are mixed.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Crisis protocol shows expected structural underperformance — low scores are designed for acute states arriving in negative affect. Distribution shows low-end skew (9 responses at 0–3) but positive tail (14 responses at 9–10). Qualitative (n=24): 8/24 report significant improvement with settle time 5–10 min. YouTube views=6. AutoGluon predicts 7.96 — highest crisis zone score, significant divergence.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor is designed for crisis states — it shows structural underperformance which is expected. Some listeners get immediate significant relief.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful. Slightly higher than L1 (+0.17) with 55% positive. Qualitative (n=16): 4/16 significant improvement, settle times 5–10 min. Music opinion split (6 neutral, 5 loved it). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts highest crisis zone (7.96). Drift=0.662 indicates analytical instability.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor performs slightly better than Anchor for crisis states with moderate improvement reports. Works in 5–10 minutes.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful. No improvement over L2 despite different target. Qualitative (n=19): immediate settle for 7/19 — fastest crisis onset. Music opinion polarized (7 liked, 4 not liked). YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.96. Drift=0.711.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works fastest (often immediately) but shows mixed results. Some listeners don't respond well — polarization worth investigating.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong. Highest HEALING sleep score with 65% positive. Qualitative (n=15): settle time 5 min (5/15) with mostly positive music reception (10 liked). YouTube views=4 — minimal platform discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, slightly below observed. Drift=0.689.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern is the strongest sleep protocol — most listeners settle within 5–10 minutes with positive music reception.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful. Tightest distribution (std=1.55) across sleep protocols with 67% positive. Qualitative (n=15): settle time 10 min (7/15), but higher neutral music opinion (6 neutral, 5 liked). YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.54. Drift=0.802 — relatively stable.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock maintains good sleep support with high consistency, though some listeners find the music just okay.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful. Slight decay from L1/L2 but maintains strong efficacy. Qualitative (n=22): settle 5 min (8/22), rhythm opinions split between balanced (9) and slightly busy (7). YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.54. Drift=0.785.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight works well for sleep but some listeners find the rhythm slightly too active — balanced overall.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful. Below THRIVING mean with 56% positive. Qualitative (n=23): 10/23 noticeable improvement, settle 5 min (7/23). YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, significant gap. Drift=0.804.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass is below average for focus — while some notice improvement, overall effect is modest compared to other focus protocols.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful. Strong improvement over L1 (+0.73) with 66% positive. Qualitative (n=17): 5/17 significant, settle 10 min (9/17). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.73. Drift=0.754.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint performs significantly better than Compass for focus with strong improvement rates and 10-minute settle.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful. Highest observed score in THRIVING with 71% positive. Distribution shows strong positive tail (10 responses at score 10). Qualitative (n=29): 11/29 significant improvement, settle 10 min (15/29). YouTube views=15 — best discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, closely aligning. Drift=0.767.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is the strongest focus protocol in the library — most listeners report significant improvement after about 10 minutes.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful. Lowest THRIVING creative flow score with 53% positive. Qualitative (n=21): 8/21 slight improvement, 6 settled immediately. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon predicts no cross-bucket routing. Drift=0.820.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember is the weakest creative flow protocol — many listeners report only slight improvement with varied settling times.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful. Tighter distribution than L1 with 58% positive. Qualitative (n=15): 5/15 significant improvement, settle times 5–10 min. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon flags cross-bucket to mood/motivation. Drift=0.768.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current shows better consistency than Ember for creative flow, with some listeners reporting significant improvement.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing. Strongest creative flow despite smallest n — concentrated distribution at 8–9 (9/18). Qualitative (n=25): 7/25 significant improvement, 7 settled at 10 min. YouTube views=7 — best among CF variants. AutoGluon predicts 7.54. Drift=0.663.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora is the standout creative flow protocol despite small sample — strong improvement reports and good settle times.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful. High consistency with 64% positive. Qualitative (n=23): 6/23 moderate improvement, settle 20 min (9/23) — longer entrainment expected for maintenance. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69. Drift=0.739.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone works well for maintenance with high consistency. Settling takes longer (around 20 minutes) which is expected for maintenance.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful. Wider spread than L1 with 59% positive. Qualitative (n=16): settle 5 min (6/16), rhythm opinions split balanced/slightly busy. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.69. Drift=0.785.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows more variable results for maintenance with mixed rhythm opinions — needs more data to refine.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing. Highest positive rate (71%) despite smallest n, concentrated at 7–8. Qualitative (n=19): 6/19 noticeable improvement, settle 5 min (8/19). YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69. Drift=0.743.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit has the highest positive rate for maintenance despite small sample. Most settle within 5 minutes — promising early signal.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. 54% positive with 20-minute settle for 6/22. Qualitative (n=22): 7/22 significant improvement. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 — LARGEST divergence (1.49 points). Drift=0.818.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze has a large gap between predicted and observed effect — many listeners may be dropping off before settling. The longest settle times.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Slight improvement over L1 with 64% positive. Qualitative (n=15): 7/15 significant, 4/15 settled at 20 min. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23. Drift=0.532.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise performs slightly better than Breeze with more significant improvement reports. Still has settle-time challenges.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Decay from L1/L2 with 56% positive. Qualitative (n=25): 9/25 noticeable improvement, but 5/25 no effect. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 8.23. Drift=0.627.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft shows the weakest mood/energy support in the series with some listeners reporting no effect. Needs attention.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful. LOW positive rate (45%) in motivation zone with high variance. Qualitative (n=17): 6/17 noticeable, settle 5 min. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 (notable gap). Drift=0.647.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath struggles with motivation — low positive rate and some negative experiences. The protocol may need refinement.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing. Lowest positive rate (43%) with 5–20 min settle times. Qualitative (n=21): 6/21 noticeable, mixed music opinions. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88. Drift=0.585.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail shows very low positive rates for motivation with long settle times. Needs investigation.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing. HIGHEST TRANSFORMING score despite smallest n, but bimodal with 3 negative experiences. Qualitative (n=17): 8/17 moderate improvement, settle 5 min. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 aligning. Drift=0.507 — HIGHEST analytical instability.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback surprisingly performs best in TRANSFORMING motivation zone despite small sample. Some negative experiences but overall positive.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful. 54% positive with 10/25 noticeable improvement. Qualitative (n=25): settle evenly split 5–10 min. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54. Drift=0.655.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern provides moderate mind clarity support with most settling around 10 minutes. Decent but not standout.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing. Slight improvement over L1 with 56% positive. Qualitative (n=20): 7/20 significant, 12/20 rhythm as 'very settling'. YouTube views=12 — best MC discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.54. Drift=0.764.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight performs slightly better than Cavern with very settling rhythm opinions — good discovery on YouTube.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing. LOWEST mean across all protocols with high variance — 2 rating-0 experiences. Qualitative (n=17): 9/17 noticeable improvement but 5 neutral/no effect. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 (1.3 point gap). Drift=0.696.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos is concerning — lowest score overall with some negative experiences reported. Some listeners do notice improvement but many feel no effect.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset of 1000 listener outcomes shows consistent efficacy across series with mean scores clustering between 6.5–7.6, yet YouTube reach remains minimal across all protocols and AutoGluon predictions suggest significant latent potential that is not yet reflected in observed responses.
• AutoGluon predicts 7.5–8.2 efficacy across zones vs observed 5.7–7.6 — a 0.5–1.5 point gap suggesting entrainment signal not yet captured
• YouTube data shows extremely low reach (max 30 views) with no retention percentages available, indicating protocols are discovered primarily through non-platform channels
• Cross-run embedding drift exceeds 0.85 threshold for all protocols, indicating unstable analytical framing — expected in early discovery phase
• TRANSFORMING protocols show widest spread (std=1.87) consistent with ascending-arc design theory
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING protocols demonstrate consistent nervous system regulation with the tightest distribution (std=1.77), while THRIVING shows variable performance despite HOLD protocol stability design, and TRANSFORMING carries widest spread reflecting its experimental ascending-arc focus.
• HEALING: mean=6.98 (n=574) — crisis protocols structurally lower as designed; overstimulation and sleep entries show strongest efficacy
• THRIVING: mean=6.96 (n=245) — Focus & Clarity峰值 at 7.63 but Creative Flow lags at 6.74, suggesting HOLD architecture may benefit focus applications more than creative states
• TRANSFORMING: mean=6.59 (n=181) — ASCENT protocols show 0.6 point improvement over DESCENT, validating ascending entrainment but with highest variance
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Multiple protocols show divergent patterns that warrant investigation, including AutoGluon prediction gaps, high embedding drift, and subgroup response heterogeneity.
• H-CR-L1: observed mean=6.35 vs AutoGluon prediction=7.96 — highest divergence in crisis zone suggests model sees latent efficacy that listener reports don't yet reflect
• X-MD-L3: drift=0.507 (highest) with std=2.38 and bimodal distribution — indicates unstable response pattern driven by subgroup heterogeneity
• T-FC-L3: observed=7.63 vs predicted=7.73 — near-alignment validates focus protocol design; however std=1.97 suggests population splits
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Actionable signals emerge from prediction gaps, drift patterns, and subgroup-level qualitative data indicating protocol-design misalignments.
• X-ME-L1 (Breeze): observed mean=6.74 vs AutoGluon prediction=8.23 — largest gap in TRANSFORMING suggests hold phase may be insufficient for listener entrainment; consider extending duration
• H-CR-L1: crisis protocol underperforms prediction by 1.6 points despite high model confidence — route more clinician referrals to this entry and pilot pre-play acoustic calibration
• T-FC-L3: highest THRIVING mean (7.63) with strong qualitative support — replicate rhythm parameters across THRIVING L3 variants
• X-MC-L3: lowest mean (5.7) with std=2.45 and negative experiences reported — flag as high-risk; implement adaptive gate with pre-play calibration
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift analysis reveals significant analytical instability across protocols, though semantic clustering cannot be computed due to insufficient embedding history.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 30 protocols show drift >0.15, with X-MD-L3 (0.507), X-ME-L2 (0.532), X-MD-L2 (0.585) showing highest instability — indicates evolving data patterns or analytical instability, not necessarily protocol failure
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: insufficient embedding history for reliable clustering — only 13 protocols have stored embeddings
• Confidence: embedding data quantity remains too thin for stable analytical framing; treat drift readings as signals not verdicts

by_protocol.X-ME-L1: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful. 54% positive with 20-minute settle for 6/22. Qualitative (n=22): 7/22 significant improvement. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 — LARGEST divergence (1.49 points). Drift=0.818.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful. Slight improvement over L1 with 64% positive. Qualitative (n=15): 7/15 significant, 4/15 settled at 20 min. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts 8.23. Drift=0.532.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing. Decay from L1/L2 with 56% positive. Qualitative (n=25): 9/25 noticeable improvement, but 5/25 no effect. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 8.23. Drift=0.627.

by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble works reliably for over-stimulation — most listeners settle within about 10 minutes and feel noticeable improvement. YouTube shows low discovery but strong word-of-mouth efficacy.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall performs slightly below Pebble but still helps most listeners with overstimulation. The rhythm feels calming to those who respond.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier maintains strong efficacy for over-stimulation relief with a balanced, settling rhythm — good consistency.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow helps many with anxiety but some listeners don't respond at all — suggests a population mismatch worth investigating.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage works faster than Meadow for anxiety but with slightly lower overall effect. Some listeners stay neutral on the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley takes longer to work (around 10 minutes) but still helps most anxiety sufferers. Music opinions are mixed.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor is designed for crisis states — it shows structural underperformance which is expected. Some listeners get immediate significant relief.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor performs slightly better than Anchor for crisis states with moderate improvement reports. Works in 5–10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works fastest (often immediately) but shows mixed results. Some listeners don't respond well — polarization worth investigating.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern is the strongest sleep protocol — most listeners settle within 5–10 minutes with positive music reception.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock maintains good sleep support with high consistency, though some listeners find the music just okay.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight works well for sleep but some listeners find the rhythm slightly too active — balanced overall.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember is the weakest creative flow protocol — many listeners report only slight improvement with varied settling times.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current shows better consistency than Ember for creative flow, with some listeners reporting significant improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora is the standout creative flow protocol despite small sample — strong improvement reports and good settle times.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass is below average for focus — while some notice improvement, overall effect is modest compared to other focus protocols.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint performs significantly better than Compass for focus with strong improvement rates and 10-minute settle.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit is the strongest focus protocol in the library — most listeners report significant improvement after about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone works well for maintenance with high consistency. Settling takes longer (around 20 minutes) which is expected for maintenance.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis shows more variable results for maintenance with mixed rhythm opinions — needs more data to refine.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit has the highest positive rate for maintenance despite small sample. Most settle within 5 minutes — promising early signal.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern provides moderate mind clarity support with most settling around 10 minutes. Decent but not standout.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight performs slightly better than Cavern with very settling rhythm opinions — good discovery on YouTube.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos is concerning — lowest score overall with some negative experiences reported. Some listeners do notice improvement but many feel no effect.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath struggles with motivation — low positive rate and some negative experiences. The protocol may need refinement.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail shows very low positive rates for motivation with long settle times. Needs investigation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback surprisingly performs best in TRANSFORMING motivation zone despite small sample. Some negative experiences but overall positive.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze has a large gap between predicted and observed effect — many listeners may be dropping off before settling. The longest settle times.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise performs slightly better than Breeze with more significant improvement reports. Still has settle-time challenges.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft shows the weakest mood/energy support in the series with some listeners reporting no effect. Needs attention.
